text:
Large language models, or LLMs, are a type of artificial intelligence that processes and generates human-like text. They work by analysing huge amounts of written material, known as training data, which includes books, articles, and websites from the internet. During training, the model learns patterns in language, such as word relationships and sentence structures. This allows it to predict the next word in a sequence when given an input.

Users interact with these models through prompt engineering, which means carefully writing questions or instructions to get useful responses. LLMs have impressive capabilities, such as answering questions, writing stories, and translating languages. However, they also have limits. They do not truly understand meaning; they can produce incorrect or biased information, and they rely entirely on their training data, without real-world knowledge or awareness.
